title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Language Lens: Persistence Defaults
summary: A server dispatches the next request before consuming the current body, writes responses out of order, reuses connections after framing errors, and waits forever for idle peers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the persistence defaults increment in persistent-connection request and response state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
Round-trip supported messages through independent parse and serialize paths while preserving semantics, limits, and declared normalization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Persistence Defaults · Theory Persistence Defaults
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4065 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Persistence Defaults contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Persistence Defaults · Theory Persistence Defaults.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for determine http/1.0 and http/1.1 persistence from version, connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy. For Predict Persistence Defaults · Theory Persistence Defaults, keep Persistence Defaults connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
